storybook-contract-a1.task:
Make the Storybook variant system renderable and persistable. This is a 3-file change scoped entirely to src/storybook/.

OBJECTIVE: Extend the Story trait and StoryVariant struct so that variants can be rendered individually and selections can be persisted to disk. All existing Story implementations must continue to compile unchanged.

CONCRETE CHANGES:

1. src/storybook/story.rs — Replace the entire file contents with:
   - Add StorySurface enum (Component, Footer, Input, Header, Shell, ActionDialog, TurnCard, FullPrompt) with Default=Component and a label() method
   - Extend Story trait: add surface() -> StorySurface (default Component), add render_variant(&self, variant: &StoryVariant) -> AnyElement (default falls back to render())
   - Add id: String field to StoryVariant, plus new(), default_named(), description(), with_prop() builder methods
   - Add StoryVariant::stable_id() that returns id if non-empty, else slugifies name (lowercase, non-alphanumeric->dash, collapse consecutive dashes, trim), else 'default'
   - Add unit test: stable_id_falls_back_to_slugified_name — 'Script Kit Branded' -> 'script-kit-branded'
   - Keep the existing variants() default but change it to return vec![StoryVariant::default_named('default', 'Default')]

2. src/storybook/selection.rs — Create new file:
   - StorySelectionStore struct with selections: HashMap<String, String>, serde rename_all camelCase
   - selected_variant(&self, story_id) -> Option<&str> and set_selected_variant(&mut self, story_id, variant_id)
   - selection_store_path() -> PathBuf using crate::setup::get_kit_path().join('design-explorer-selections.json')
   - load_story_selections() and save_story_selections() with anyhow::Context on all fs ops
   - Unit test: story_selection_store_roundtrips — set a variant, serialize, deserialize, assert round-trip

3. src/storybook/mod.rs — Add 'mod selection;' and pub use the new exports: load_story_selections, save_story_selections, StorySelectionStore, StorySurface

KEY ANCHORS:
- src/storybook/story.rs — current Story trait and StoryVariant (no id field, no surface, no render_variant)
- src/storybook/mod.rs — current module exports (browser, layout, registry, story)
- src/storybook/browser.rs — uses Story trait (must still compile after changes)
- src/stories/ — 20+ Story implementations (must all still compile with new trait)
- crate::setup::get_kit_path() — used for the selection store path
- CLAUDE.md rules: use anyhow::Context on fallible ops, serde rename_all camelCase for protocol structs

BACKWARD COMPAT: The new surface() and render_variant() methods have defaults, so existing stories that only implement id/name/category/render/variants will compile unchanged. The new StoryVariant::id field defaults to empty string, so existing StoryVariant::default() still works.

VERIFICATION: cargo check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test

report.taskSummary: Extended the storybook contract with story surfaces, per-variant rendering, stable variant IDs, and a persisted story selection store while preserving backward compatibility for existing stories.

list-shell-scaffold-a1.task:
Add two new helpers to src/components/prompt_layout_shell.rs and their regression tests. This is a Rust GPUI project — read CLAUDE.md for conventions.

OBJECTIVE: Add render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold() and render_minimal_list_prompt_shell() to src/components/prompt_layout_shell.rs, plus source-regression tests in the existing prompt_layout_shell_tests module.

CONCRETE STEPS:
1. Read src/components/prompt_layout_shell.rs fully first.
2. Insert these two functions directly AFTER the render_simple_prompt_shell() function (after line ~327, before render_simple_hint_strip):

#[allow(dead_code)]
pub(crate) fn render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold(
    header: impl IntoElement,
    content: impl IntoElement,
    hints: impl crate::components::hint_strip::IntoHints,
    leading: Option<AnyElement>,
) -> Div {
    div()
        .w_full()
        .h_full()
        .flex()
        .flex_col()
        .child(
            div()
                .w_full()
                .px(px(crate::ui::chrome::HEADER_PADDING_X))
                .py(px(crate::ui::chrome::HEADER_PADDING_Y))
                .flex()
                .flex_row()
                .items_center()
                .child(header),
        )
        .child(crate::components::SectionDivider::new())
        .child(
            div()
                .flex()
                .flex_col()
                .flex_1()
                .min_h(px(0.))
                .w_full()
                .overflow_hidden()
                .child(content),
        )
        .child(render_simple_hint_strip(hints, leading))
}

#[allow(dead_code)]
pub(crate) fn render_minimal_list_prompt_shell(
    radius: f32,
    vibrancy_bg: Option<Rgba>,
    header: impl IntoElement,
    content: impl IntoElement,
    hints: impl crate::components::hint_strip::IntoHints,
    leading: Option<AnyElement>,
) -> Div {
    render_simple_prompt_shell(
        radius,
        vibrancy_bg,
        render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold(header, content, hints, leading),
        None,
    )
}

3. Then add re-exports in src/components/mod.rs. Find the existing block:
pub(crate) use prompt_layout_shell::{
    render_hint_strip_leading_text, render_simple_hint_strip, render_simple_prompt_shell,
};
And add render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold and render_minimal_list_prompt_shell to that list.

4. Add these two tests inside the existing #[cfg(test)] mod prompt_layout_shell_tests block (at the end, before the closing brace):

    #[test]
    fn render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold_uses_shared_tokens_and_footer() {
        let fn_start = SHELL_SOURCE
            .find("fn render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold(")
            .expect("function must exist");
        let fn_body = &SHELL_SOURCE[fn_start..];

        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("HEADER_PADDING_X"),
            "shared list scaffold must own HEADER_PADDING_X"
        );
        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("HEADER_PADDING_Y"),
            "shared list scaffold must own HEADER_PADDING_Y"
        );
        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("SectionDivider::new()"),
            "shared list scaffold must own the divider"
        );
        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("render_simple_hint_strip("),
            "shared list scaffold must own the hint strip footer"
        );
        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("flex_1()") && fn_body.contains("min_h(px(0."),
            "shared list scaffold must own the flex content contract"
        );
    }

    #[test]
    fn render_minimal_list_prompt_shell_delegates_to_simple_shell() {
        let fn_start = SHELL_SOURCE
            .find("fn render_minimal_list_prompt_shell(")
            .expect("function must exist");
        let fn_body = &SHELL_SOURCE[fn_start..];

        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("render_simple_prompt_shell("),
            "shared list shell must delegate to render_simple_prompt_shell"
        );
        assert!(
            fn_body.contains("render_minimal_list_prompt_scaffold("),
            "shared list shell must wrap the scaffold"
        );
    }

5. Run verification: cargo check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test prompt_layout_shell

KEY FILES:
- src/components/prompt_layout_shell.rs — the file to edit (read it first)
- src/components/mod.rs — add re-exports
- src/ui/chrome/tokens.rs — HEADER_PADDING_X, HEADER_PADDING_Y constants
- src/components/hint_strip.rs — IntoHints trait
- src/components/section_divider.rs — SectionDivider::new()

CONVENTIONS (from CLAUDE.md):
- Use #[allow(dead_code)] on new helpers not yet called
- pub(crate) visibility for cross-module internals
- No unwrap() in production code (tests can use .expect())
- cargo check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test must pass
list-shell-scaffold-a1.notes: done. exit=0. final=.ai/logs/list-shell-scaffold-a1.final.md. See final message file.

report.taskSummary: Added minimal list prompt scaffold/shell helpers, re-exported them, and added regression tests covering their shared layout contract and delegation.
